rationale: CI's exact test job (`go test -short -race -coverprofile=... ./...`) FAILS on this branch at 21374bb8: internal/lint.TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks (the ADR-0030 enforcement-boundary AST lint, unchanged by this spec's diff) flags internal/approve/impl.go:17 for importing internal/gitutil directly, a banned import for internal/{validate,approve,complete,state,phase}. Bead 2 added `gitutil.IsAncestor`/`gitutil.BranchExists` to approve/impl.go (confirmed new via `git diff origin/main..21374bb8 -- internal/approve/impl.go`) with no `boundary-allowlisted:` marker and no update to boundary_test.go's doctrine/list. This is reproducible in a clean checkout (pure AST walk, no environment dependency) and directly falsifies the BRIEF's 'no stray fence violations' / ADR-0030 fitness claim -- other panel slots (O2, F3) APPROVED after checking only the touched-package shortlist, which excludes internal/lint, and missed it. All other CI jobs pass: build clean; vet clean; gofmt -l empty; redaction-gate (go test -race ./internal/redact/... ./internal/version/...) green; go mod tidy clean; `mindspec validate spec 115` all-checks-passed. One other test failure, internal/instruct.TestRun_IdleNoBeads, is confirmed pre-existing/environment-only (bead z4ps: passes in a fresh CI clone, fails only when run inside an active mindspec worktree) -- not a blocker. golangci-lint locally flags 2 gosec G115 in internal/journal/lock_unix.go, confirmed pre-existing/untouched by this diff and tracked as bead 8ud6 ('CI currently tolerates it, version skew on pinned golangci-lint-action') -- not a regression, not a blocker. No fence-violation strings (raw git merge, --no-verify, MINDSPEC_ALLOW_MAIN, settable MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL bypass) found in the production diff. The --allow-doc-skew disposition is legitimate: no .mindspec/docs/domains/ path exists (the real dir is .mindspec/domains/, untouched in substance by this diff except an OWNERSHIP.yaml addition), and the four domain architecture/interfaces.md files contain no claim contradicted by the new gate.
concrete_changes_required: Fix internal/lint.TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks (verify with `go test ./internal/lint/... -run TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks -v`, currently FAILS at 21374bb8): internal/approve/impl.go directly imports internal/gitutil (gitutil.IsAncestor, gitutil.BranchExists), which is a banned import for ADR-0030 enforcement packages per the pre-existing, unchanged boundary_test.go doctrine. Resolve by ONE of: (a) add a reviewed `// boundary-allowlisted: <reason>` file-doc marker to impl.go documenting why the C+B design's direct gitutil.BranchExists/IsAncestor reads are a sanctioned, reviewed exception to the no-git-leak rule; or (b) update boundary_test.go's bannedImports/enforcementPkgs doctrine (with an ADR-0030 amendment) to reflect that internal/approve may read via gitutil directly for this narrow bool/ancestry-check purpose; or (c) route the two calls through internal/executor instead, preserving the existing boundary invariant untouched. Whichever direction is chosen, `go test ./...` (the exact CI command) must go green including internal/lint.

findings.title: internal/lint.TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks FAILS at 21374bb8 -- CI test job would be RED
findings.detail: `go test -short -race ./...` (verbatim CI `test` job command from .github/workflows/ci.yml) fails: `internal/lint/boundary_test.go:141: boundary violations in enforcement packages (1): internal/approve/impl.go:17 (<file-scope>): banned import "github.com/mrmaxsteel/mindspec/internal/gitutil>"`. Verified: (1) boundary_test.go is byte-unchanged by the whole-spec diff (`git diff origin/main..21374bb8 --stat -- internal/lint/` empty); (2) the gitutil import at impl.go:17 is new, added by Bead 2 (`+	"github.com/mrmaxsteel/mindspec/internal/gitutil"` in the diff) for `implIsAncestorFn = gitutil.IsAncestor` / `implBranchExistsFn = gitutil.BranchExists`; (3) impl.go has no `boundary-allowlisted:` file-doc marker; (4) `internal/approve` is explicitly one of the five ADR-0030 enforcement packages the test walks (validate, approve, complete, state, phase); (5) reran the exact test standalone -- confirmed FAIL, deterministic, no environment dependency (pure AST parse of static files). This directly contradicts the design's own claimed ADR-0030 fitness and would fail CI on a fresh, clean checkout -- not an artifact of this reviewer's environment.

findings.title: internal/instruct.TestRun_IdleNoBeads failure is pre-existing/environment-only, not a spec-115 regression
findings.detail: Full suite run showed this FAIL, but internal/instruct is untouched by the spec-115 diff, and the failure mode matches bead mindspec-z4ps exactly (fake .git in setupRunTestProject doesn't anchor test resolution, which walks up to the REAL enclosing worktree -- this worktree is itself mid-review-mode -- and emits impl-approve guidance instead of 'No Active Work'). z4ps's own text says it 'passes in fresh CI checkout -- CI runs in a clean clone, not a mindspec worktree.' Confirmed by rerunning in isolation: same failure, same root cause. Not a merge blocker; already tracked (P3, open).

findings.title: golangci-lint gosec G115 findings are pre-existing, tracked, and CI-tolerated (not a regression)
findings.detail: Local golangci-lint v2.11.1 flags 2 gosec G115 integer-overflow findings in internal/journal/lock_unix.go:29,34 (int(f.Fd()) in syscall.Flock). This file is untouched by the whole spec-115 diff (`git log origin/main..21374bb8 -- internal/journal/lock_unix.go` empty). Confirmed via `bd show 8ud6`: filed 2026-06-17, explicitly 'pre-existing (on main; not introduced by any recent spec) ... CI currently tolerates it (version skew on the pinned golangci-lint-action)' -- CI pins golangci-lint-action v2.1, an older gosec ruleset than the local v2.11.1 binary used here. Not a spec-115 regression; the CI `lint` job would pass as pinned.
